provenance.source_corpus: Canada's Rough Fescue Grasslands: A Trial Restoration Project in Alberta is Yielding Encouraging Results

source_spans.text: Raking the donor fescue grassland helps reduce thatch and allows for thicker cuts of sod with greater root mass.
Raking the donor fescue grassland helps reduce thatch and allows for thicker cuts of sod with greater root mass.
Raking the donor fescue grassland helps reduce thatch and allows for thicker cuts of sod with greater root mass.

nodes.name: Raking dead grass with garden rakes before cutting

source_spans.text: we resolved the problem by raking the dead grass with garden rakes before cutting.
The raking also allowed us to cut deeper into the soil and gain a greater sod thickness and root mass.
The raking also allowed us to cut deeper into the soil and gain a greater sod thickness and root mass.
The raking also allowed us to cut deeper into the soil and gain a greater sod thickness and root mass.
This can be attributed to a greatly diminished thatch cover due to raking on the reconstructed sites, which allows more rapid warming of the soils.
This can be attributed to a greatly diminished thatch cover due to raking on the reconstructed sites, which allows more rapid warming of the soils.
Spring green-up occurred approximately two weeks earlier on the resodded site than on comparable sites in the undisturbed control site
Spring green-up occurred approximately two weeks earlier on the resodded site than on comparable sites in the undisturbed control site
the plot on the restored site that I burned in the early spring greened-up even earlier

nodes.name: Mowing in the spring covered with a layer of clippings

source_spans.text: sites mowed in the spring and covered with a layer of clippings greened-up slowly.
mowing in the spring strongly favored wheatgrass and bluegrass species and disfavored rough fescue.
mowing in the spring strongly favored wheatgrass and bluegrass species and disfavored rough fescue.
mowing in the spring strongly favored wheatgrass and bluegrass species and disfavored rough fescue.
Some herbs, such as goldenrod (Solidago spp.), bluebell (Campanula spp.), and vetch (Lathyrus spp.), also benefitted from mowing.
transplanting clearly favored shallow-rooted species over deep-rooted species.
transplanting clearly favored shallow-rooted species over deep-rooted species.
transplanting clearly favored shallow-rooted species over deep-rooted species.
In general, herbs are favored over grasses, probably in part for the same reason as suggested for the grasses.
The annuals have become less abundant in subsequent years as gaps between the sod have filled with leaf litter and the native species have infilled by lateral growth.
The use of the Bobcat for this purpose made the whole sodding process proceed much more quickly with much less effort
The use of the Bobcat for this purpose made the whole sodding process proceed much more quickly with much less effort

We experienced cutting difficulty because the cutter drive wheel slipped on the thick thatch from previous years of growth and non-use, but we resolved the problem by raking the dead grass with garden rakes before cutting.
